CREATE TABLE test_key_mapping (id INT PRIMARY KEY, key_value VARCHAR(255));
CREATE FUNCTION test_sinv_create_key_mapping() RETURNS VOID AS $$ BEGIN sinv_create_key_mapping('test_key_mapping', 'key_value'); END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, mapping_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (mapping_id) REFERENCES test_key_mapping(id));
SELECT * FROM test_key_mapping WHERE key_value LIKE 'mapping%';
SELECT a.id, b.key_value FROM another_table a JOIN test_key_mapping b ON a.mapping_id = b.id WHERE b.key_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_key_mapping WHERE LENGTH(key_value) < 255;
INSERT INTO test_key_mapping (id, key_value) VALUES (3, NULL);
SELECT a.id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM another_table) as total_mappings FROM test_key_mapping a WHERE a.id IN (SELECT DISTINCT mapping_id FROM another_table);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS test_sinv_create_key_mapping CASCADE;
